description: Apollo 17 Flight Journal - Day 6, part 3: Solo operations 1

tokens: And, America, I have a Pan Camera photo PAD whenever you're ready for it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The LM has a Stay for T-2.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, America, we're ready for the High Gain to Auto.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: It's a good thing it's in the sextant because you can't see it in the telescope.
Because the Earth is in the field of view.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: If those look good to you, I'll torque at 20.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I think I'll go ahead and use a shaft to 00.12, and a 57.470.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: It's all - it looks good, Ron.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Pan Camera Power is coming Off.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And the VHF is Off to Receive Only - is Off.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: SM/AC Power's On, and those circuit breakers are In.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Barber pole, and then a gray.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: IR; barber pole, and a gray.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: UV Cover; a barber pole, and a gray.
And we'll try timing the old Mapping Camera.
See how she does this time.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: We wanted to take 11 into A, and stow number 9, which you're going to take out, into A-9.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The old lithium hydroxide canister's changed.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And GNC has noticed that you've got the Optics not in Zero, and next time you're down that way, it would make everybody feel better if you'd put it there.
It's not - not something that you have to do right away.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The CapCom role shifts from Ken Mattingly to Robert Overmyer.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: 113:44:26 -This is Apollo Control at 113 hours, 44 minutes Ground Elapsed Time in the mission of Apollo 17.

Some refined numbers on the landing site for Challenger: In degrees, minutes and seconds, the latitude has been pinned down to 20 degrees, 9 minutes, 50.5 seconds north; longitude 30 degrees, 46 minutes, 19.3 minutes - or seconds east, which is about 639 meters east of the designated landing point - the premission landing point.
For those newsmen who have the landing site with the grid coordinates on it, that corresponds to DN.0 by 84.2.
At 113:45 Ground Elapsed Time, this is Apollo Control.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: 113:48:59 -This is Apollo Control at 113 hours, 48 minutes Ground Elapsed Time.
We're estimating a change of shift, if you will Press Conference, with the landing team flight director, Gerry Griffin, in the small briefing room at 3:00 pm.
To repeat, estimated Press Conference in about 20 minutes, approximately, 18 minutes to be exact, in the small Briefing Room in Building 1.
Some 42 minutes away from acquisition as the Command Module, America, comes around on revolution 14.
Meanwhile on the surface, to repeat the estimated landing site.
It is right down the line on the Ground Track.
However some 369 meters east or short of the predesignated, premission landing point.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: 114:11:10 -This is Apollo control at 114 hours, 11 minutes Ground Elapsed Time into the mission of Apollo 17.
Commander Gene Cernan's heart rate during the descent and landing ranged from 102 average during the descent portion of the landing to 128 at actual touchdown.
There's a change of shift briefing that will start momentarily in the small briefing room in building 1.
We'll take down the broadcast line at this time, tape any conversation during that period and play it back at the conclusion of the briefing which starts now.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: The audio recordings of the CSM solo mission that are currently available to the Journal cease at this point.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay; just about have you, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Looks like we have you for good now.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Bus Ties are Off; Pyro Bus Ties are Open.
Let's see about Bus A and B.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay; Relay Bus Bat B is Open - coming Open.
Bat Charge to Alpha and then to Bravo.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Is the time for the Lunar Sounder stuff?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Lunar Sounder Operate is in Standby.
Off to center; not to Heaters.
And the Mode is going to VHF.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: We're ready for the HF Antenna Extend test, and, just for your information, White Sands got one of the rockets off.
The other one was a No Go.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: We'd like to try Antenna 1 again.
We'd like you to go Extend for barber pole plus 3 seconds, then Off, then Retract.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: HF for number 1 to Retract, now.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay; it's Off, and the talkback is gray.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: On that one, we followed your Retract current in for 7 seconds, and then it went into stall; so it apparently is the track - it's retracting normally up to a point, and then goes into stall.
We'd like you to press on and do Extend on HF Antenna 2.
And we're going to press on here, probably thinking like maybe we won't get the antennas in.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Okay; number 2 going to Retract, now.
Okay; it went gray - and back to Off.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Let me get the clock going here.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Hey, I think I can see a light spot down there on the landing site where they might have blown off some of that halo stuff.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Hey, it's gray now on the number 1 Extend.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay; number 2 is going to - it's Off.
And number 2 is going to Extend.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: SM/AC Power, we're going to get that Off.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: America, while we're waiting for this Lunar Sounder to operate for 2 minutes, could you - could you say again those coordinates you gave us.
I dug out the map TL 25-8, and I got the 83.3, but what was the azimuth coordinate on that, Ron?
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: I'll select, I'm going to need Bravo.
Do old High Gain, Manual and Wide.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: High Gain Antenna Power is Off.
Okay; ready for the HF part of it?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: 115:16:18 -This is Apollo Control at 115 hours, 16 minutes.
Mission Control at presently is using these coordinates as the landing point.
We will continue to try to refine that landing point further.
But, as of now, we're saying the landing point is 20 degrees, 9 minutes, 50 seconds north; 30 degrees, 46 minutes, 19 seconds east.
This would correspond to - on the map, for those of you who have grid maps, DM 8 and 82.7.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: 115:17:25 -It's estimated that the Lunar Module Challenger is on the planned north south line and approximately 1 to 200 meters east of the planned landing point.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: 115:18:35 -This is Apollo Control at 115 hours, 18 minutes.
We want to distinguish between the targeted landing point and the planned landing point.
It had been the crew's intention all along to land approximately 200 meters east or short of the targeted landing point, the point at which the computer was targeting the Challenger.
So, we believe that Challenger is approximately 300 meters east of the targeted landing point, which would put it about 100 to 200 meters east of the planned landing point.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Barber pole and then a gray.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay; Bit Rate - Bit Rate to High.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay; we're maneuvering to VHF test attitude.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I'm about ready to press ahead, if you all are?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: High Gain Antenna Power is On.
We go to Manual and Wide.
Minus about 12, and Yaw about 21 or something, 211.
Looks like it worked pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: 115:29:36 -This is Apollo Control at 115 hours, 30 minutes.
Ron Evans in America has about 14½ minutes left before Loss of Signal on the 14th revolution.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Lose a little comm here, huh?
The High Gain at minus - Manual, Wide.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And High Gain Antenna Power is going Off.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: You know, you'll never believe it.
I'm right over the edge of Orientale.
I just looked down and saw a light flash myself.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: 115:36:34 -Ron Evans has just reported seeing a light flash just to the east of crater Orientale.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: 115:45:15 -This is Apollo Control at 115 hours, 45 minutes.
We've just had a Loss of Signal with America, which has now gone behind the Moon.
We'll next acquire the Command Module at 116 hours, 30 minutes.
All going well with Evans in America.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Looks like you're with me now.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay, I've got to switch to window number 3 for end of target.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: VHBW is very high-speed black & white film, typically rated at 6,000 ASA.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: You know you look at - next to Macrobius A, there is a kind of a dark halo-type crater there.
And it doesn't have the appearance of a hummocky crater rim to it, at all.
It looks like the material just kind of spreads out all over the area, but it doesn't have a hummocky appearance to it.
I'm going to take a look at that again when I come back - on back around on the other side.
There's a small mound down in the bottom of the crater, also.
It's a domical-shaped structure in the bottom of that small crater.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: No, it still looks like that area that's blown away there is Dog November - between Dog November and Dog Papa.
And about 83.4 or something like that.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: At your convenience, High Gain to Auto.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Okay, Pan Camera Power - Let's see.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: It wasn't as bright - That pass over - This last pass - as it was the time before.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I hope you haven't - I hope you haven't spilled any; and, by the way, LMP is on the surface now, too.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Okay, Mapping Camera is going to Standby.
UV Cover is Closed; barber pole, gray.
IR Cover is Closed; barber pole, gray.
Okay, Map Camera Laser Altimeter - we'll leave that one Open.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Might made notes, so I wouldn't forget about it.
It was when you go to tunnel vent, it takes a heck of a long time to vent that tunnel.
In the simulator down there, you go to tunnel vent, and it flips right down and vents.
And I was beginning to wonder if it - if it was leaking or something.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Roger; it's in the Flight Plan.
But it's just before you're going to get busy on that orbital science photo.
You might of - you might go to the window earlier or something.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Ron, you're 5 minutes to LOS here.
And you're looking real good all around the room.
No problems with any of the systems that we can see.
And we'll be with you for another 5 minutes here.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: You've got a Lunar Sounder HF pass at 1 - starting - at 119, for a site - well, actually a 2-hour, no, 1-hour that you could probably grab some food at that time.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Roll is 187; pitch, 104; yaw, 323; rest of the PAD is not applicable.
Good old Sirius and Rigel for set stars; 133, 200, 030.
Longitude of the Moon at TIGwill be minus 160.39.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Interpretation of the PAD is as follows:Purpose:This is an abort PAD for an early return home at the end of the 26th orbit if there is a problem that would end the mission.System:The burn would use the SPS engine under the control of the Guidance and Navigation system.CSM Weight (Noun 47):37,630 pounds (17,069 kg).Pitch and yaw trim (Noun 48):+0.53° and +0.97°.Time of ignition, TIG(Noun 33):139 hours, 41 minutes, 14.32 seconds.Change in velocity (Noun 81), fps (m/s):X, +2,450.7 (+747.0); Y, -2,097.8 (-639.4); Z, -807.1 (-246.0).
These velocity components are expressed with respect to the local vertical frame of reference.Spacecraft attitude:Roll, 187°; Pitch, 104°; Yaw, 323°.

The attitude angles are given with respect to the current REFSMMAT.GDC Align stars:Stars 15, (Sirius, Alpha Canis Majoris) and 12, (Rigel, Beta Orionis) are to be used to align the gyro assemblies if it is not possible to use the guidance platform for this purpose.GDC Align angles:x, 133°; y, 200°; z, 30°.There are two additional notes.
SPS propellants are settled in their tanks by firing the plus-X thrusters on all four quads around the Service Module for 12 seconds.
At the moment of ignition, the spacecraft would be passing a longitude 160.39°W
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: And the Earthshine photo PAD is at 121:05 in the Flight Plan.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: And, Ron, we'd like the Recorder, On, on the Lunar Sounder.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Just talked to the homefront, Ron.
And I guess some of you guys will do anything to get out of - get away from putting up...
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Finally got mag ZZ on the ole Nikon.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And set the High Gain Manual and Wide, minus 74.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: 118:50:11 -The Command Module is over the landing site.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We're - It looks like we're about 30 seconds in front of Operate.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Ron, when you get a chance, we'd like H2Tank 3 Fan to Off.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: All the data so far on the Lunar Sounder has come out real good, and the HF pass is looking real good.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Gene became the first auto-mechanic on the Moon as one of the rear fenders fell off the Rover right after they deployed it, so he had to tape it on with some tape.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: You can't pack them in there, you have to - yes...
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Well, I can see Reiner Gamma real well out window 3.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay, I want to switch to window 3.
A little better to try and get some pictures.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: No [garble] for picture taking, but you need to select Omni A when you get a chance.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Well, I didn't see any slices down there that time.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Ron, you're about ready to go over the hill.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Lobachevsky is a crater on the far side.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The mag Lima Lima is on frame number 54 now.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I got to unplug you for a little bit.
I'm so tangled up in my hoses here, I'm getting strangled to death.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Houston, do you need Omni Bravo yet?
Will you give me a call when you want it?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: The following four utterances are not recorded on the audio supplied by NARA.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The NARA recording picks up from the following utterance.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Well, South Massif just went into a hole, too, so...
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Were you able to see the Sul- Sulpicius Gallus Ridge there, Ron?
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: You're about 2 minutes from T-start time, but again bias your T-start time with respect to the crater Eratosthenes.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: You're about 30 seconds from T-start time.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: 121:04:08 -Ron Evans is coming up over the crater Copernicus in his 17th revolution of the Moon.
He's right on the Flight Plan.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: You probably should be able to see Encke out there now.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Did you get that mag finished up?
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Can you still see - see things in Earthshine or is it getting pretty black down there?
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Okay; that's my last picture off.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Ron, are you out - glued to a window, or are you just eating now?
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Bat C is about, oh, 36.8 or 9, something like that [cough].
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Pyro A is 37; Pyro B is 37.
I think you want the quads now, [garble].
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And, Ron, we'd like Omni Bravo.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Ron, you're less than a minute to Lunar Sounder, Operate and Standby.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: You can go Recorder, Off, now, Ron.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Radar's coming Off, Data System is coming On.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: High Gain Antenna is going to Power.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Ron, if you're finished there, we would like the - Accept.
We got to get on monitor load, and we got a state vector for you tonight.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: The Moon's gravitational field was found to be far less uniform than expected.
Many of the large maria exhibit a higher gravitational field which is attributed to high density basalt having erupted onto the surface.
Spacecraft accelerate slightly as they approach these concentrations of mass (hence 'mascons') and decelerate as they leave.
The long-term effect on low orbits around the Moon is to gradually increase an orbit's eccentricity.
If uncorrected, the orbit's perilune will eventually intersect the surface.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: I checked out the tone booster, and it's hooked up.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Well, I guess I'd better chlorinate the old water.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Are we going to leave tank 3 - I mean, tank 2, H2Fan, On, all the time?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: H2tank 2 Fan, On, for all night.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Only thing we've got, Ron, is it looks like you've got to get the Bat B off the Charge and then - configure the comm, and you are all ready to bed down.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Ron, we just been debating here; you need to be sure to do the knobs at the minus 10 and plus 25.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Ron, everything's looking great down here.
It was a really great day, really busy.
We're sorry about that eat period; and it's been real - lot of - lot of things accomplished.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: Ron, just a reminder on the S-Band Normal, Voice, Off, when you're ready to - back out.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: We're going to drop the up-link here in 1 minute, so see you tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: Overmyer

tokens: You can call me, but I can't call you.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: 123:34:57 -Ron Evans in America began an 8-hour rest period about 10 minutes ago.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: 123:40:17 -America has just gone behind the Moon.
transcript.speaker: PAO
